code:
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """Convert a list of triples about an athlete into a fluent, combined sentence."""
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # organise triples by subject for easy lookup
    subj_map: dict[str, list[tuple[str, str]]] = {}
    for t in triples:
        subj_map.setdefault(t.subject, []).append((t.predicate, t.object))

    # helper to render a single (subject, predicate, object) with possible
    # augmentation using information from other triples
    def render(s: str, p: str, o: str) -> str:
        """Render a single triple, adding a concise relative clause when the object
        itself has useful predicates (e.g., a club’s city, manager, chairman)."""
        # ----- basic phrase construction -----
        # mapping of predicates to natural language templates
        phrase_map = {
            "birthDate": "{s} was born on {o}",
            "birthYear": "{s} was born in {o}",
            "birthPlace": "{s} was born in {o}",
            "height": "{s} is {o} meters tall",
            "weight": "{s} weighs {o}",
            "position": "{s} plays as a {o}",
            "club": "{s} plays for {o}",
            "currentclub": "{s} plays for {o}",
            "formerTeam": "{s} formerly played for {o}",
            "league": "{s} plays in the {o}",
            "manager": "{s} is managed by {o}",
            "coach": "{s} is coached by {o}",
            "draftYear": "{s} was drafted in {o}",
            "draftRound": "{s} was selected in round {o}",
            "draftPick": "{s} was the {o}th pick",
            "playerNumber": "{s} wears number {o}",
            "currentteam": "{s} currently plays for {o}",
            "debutTeam": "{s} debuted with {o}",
            "leader": "{o} is the leader of {s}",
            "ethnicGroup": "{o} are an ethnic group in {s}",
            "deathPlace": "{s} died in {o}",
            "isPartOf": "{s} is part of {o}",
            "country": "{s} is in {o}",
            "ground": "{s}'s ground is {o}",
            "owner": "{o} owns the {s}",
            "currency": "The currency of {s} is {o}",
            "demonym": "The demonym of {s} is {o}",
            "officialLanguage": "The official language of {s} is {o}",
            "language": "The language of {s} is {o}",
            "anthem": "The anthem of {s} is {o}",
            "chairman": "The chairman of {s} is {o}",
            "stadium": "The stadium of {s} is {o}",
            "universityTeam": "The university team of {s} is {o}",
            "mostChampions": "The team with most championships in {s} is {o}",
            "leaderTitle": "The leader title of {s} is {o}",
            "timeZone": "{s} is in the {o} time zone",
            "utcOffset": "{s} has UTC offset {o}",
            "areaTotal": "{s} has a total area of {o}",
            "foundingDate": "{s} was founded on {o}",
            "season": "{s} season is {o}",
        }
        if p in phrase_map:
            base = phrase_map[p].format(s=s, o=o)
        else:
            base = f"{s} {p} {o}"

        # ----- add relative clause when possible -----
        # map of predicate -> wording for relative clause
        rel_map = {
            "city": "based in",
            "ground": "located in",
            "manager": "managed by",
            "coach": "coached by",
            "chairman": "chaired by",
            "league": "competes in",
            "country": "in",
        }

        # If the object is also a subject elsewhere, try to attach the most
        # relevant secondary predicate as a relative clause.
        if o in subj_map:
            for rel_pred, wording in rel_map.items():
                for sp, so in subj_map[o]:
                    if sp == rel_pred:
                        # special handling for “city” to use “who is based in”
                        if rel_pred == "city":
                            base += f", who is {wording} {so}"
                        else:
                            base += f", which is {wording} {so}"
                        # attach only the first relevant clause
                        break
                else:
                    continue
                break

        return base

    # Determine subjects that should be omitted because they are only
    # referenced as objects and their predicates are already expressed via
    # relative clauses.
    objects_set = {t.object for t in triples}
    # predicates that we attach as relative clauses (those present in rel_map)
    rel_predicates = {"city", "ground", "manager", "coach", "chairman", "league", "country"}
    skip_subjects = {
        s for s, pred_objs in subj_map.items()
        if s in objects_set and set(p for p, _ in pred_objs).issubset(rel_predicates)
    }

    # build phrases per subject, merging multiple predicates of the same subject
    phrases = []
    for s, pred_objs in subj_map.items():
        if s in skip_subjects:
            continue
        # group objects by predicate
        pred_map: dict[str, list[str]] = {}
        for p, o in pred_objs:
            pred_map.setdefault(p, []).append(o)

        sub_parts: list[str] = []

        # handle clubs/currentclubs together
        clubs = pred_map.get("club", []) + pred_map.get("currentclub", [])
        if clubs:
            if len(clubs) == 1:
                sub_parts.append(f"{s} plays for {clubs[0]}")
            else:
                club_list = ", ".join(clubs[:-1]) + f", and {clubs[-1]}"
                sub_parts.append(f"{s} plays for {club_list}")

        # handle formerTeam specially – may need city/country info
        former = pred_map.get("formerTeam", [])
        if former:
            ft = former[0]  # assume only one
            # try to enrich with city/country of the team
            city_parts = []
            for rel_pred in ("city", "country"):
                for sp, so in subj_map.get(ft, []):
                    if sp == rel_pred:
                        city_parts.append(so)
            if city_parts:
                location = ", ".join(city_parts)
                sub_parts.append(f"formerly played for {ft}, based in {location}")
            else:
                sub_parts.append(f"formerly played for {ft}")

        # other predicates
        for p, objs in pred_map.items():
            if p in ("club", "currentclub", "formerTeam"):
                continue
            for o in objs:
                sub_parts.append(render(s, p, o))

        # combine parts for this subject
        if sub_parts:
            if len(sub_parts) == 1:
                phrases.append(sub_parts[0])
            else:
                combined = "; ".join(sub_parts[:-1]) + f"; and {sub_parts[-1]}"
                phrases.append(combined)

    # finally join all subject‑level phrases into one sentence
    if not phrases:
        return ""
    if len(phrases) == 1:
        return phrases[0] + "."
    else:
        return " ".join(phrases) + "."

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
